_comment: Canonical config for the local Qwen worker pipeline. Referenced by the MCP server (Phase 2+). Recorded at execution time 2026-06-30.

runner.notes: Models forced to E: via OLLAMA_MODELS (User env) AND the Ollama desktop app's db.sqlite settings.models column (the GUI overrides the env var when it spawns the server). C: is space-constrained.

offload.notes: Ollama auto-places attention/shared layers on the GPU (VRAM sits just under the card's cap) and streams MoE experts from system RAM. GPU util is low (memory-bound, expected for MoE with few active params).
offload.vram_tight_policy: DO NOT downgrade the quant (user directive: stay on Q4_K_M). If VRAM approaches OOM, the lever is CONTEXT SIZE: cap num_ctx (KV cache grows with context). Ollama default context is modest; only raise num_ctx deliberately and watch nvidia-smi.

keep_alive.rationale: First request loads the model (~48 GB, cold ~55 s); requests within keep_alive reuse the resident model (warm ~0.1 s). Measured: 0.13 s warm load.
keep_alive.do_not_use_infinite: A warm model holds the whole GPU, which blocks other GPU work. 30m keeps it instant during a coding session but auto-unloads when idle so the GPU returns to other apps. GPU is taken in turns, not shared.

baseline_perf.note: Cold load is a one-time cost per model load; keep_alive keeps it warm. Warm throughput ~50-58 tok/s on small generations.

providers._comment: Workers Claude can delegate to via the generic delegate(provider, role, task) MCP tool. Add a provider here + a handler in src/providers.py.

qwen._note: Uses runner.host + worker_model.tag + keep_alive.value above. Free, local, owns the GPU.

gemini._note: Two-tier Vertex worker (used by the `delegate` tool via google-genai). DO NOT put secrets/project/model-ids here — this file is committed. Set project, credentials_file (your Vertex service-account JSON), models.flash/pro (bare Vertex ids, e.g. gemini-2.5-pro), and enabled=true in config/qwen.local.json (gitignored). Copy config/qwen.local.example.json. The delegate/assign `model` arg picks 'flash' (routine) or 'pro' (hard) per task; empty = default_model.

openai._note: Codex/GPT. No key yet - optional future slot. Set OPENAI_API_KEY + model, then enabled=true.

gate._comment: §6.1 mechanical gate + worker->worker auto-retry. Runs entirely in-pipeline (no Claude tokens): checks a worker's output, and on failure bounces the verbatim error back to the worker up to max_retries times. Python is reliable in isolation; TS is off by default (snippets miss imports); C++ (UE) is deferred to the §9 host-harness (full builds too slow per-task).

python._note: Add "ruff" to checks to also lint (only fails if ruff is installed in the venv).

typescript._note: ON for SELF-CONTAINED TS (pinned typescript in package.json; invoked via `node`). `tsc --noEmit --strict` type-checks a standalone snippet in ms-to-seconds and catches real type errors. The default path is relative to the repo root; set an absolute path in config/qwen.local.json if the server's working directory differs. LIMITATION: a snippet that imports from a specific PROJECT won't resolve those imports → such a snippet fails the gate. For project-coupled TS, use `assign(...)` instead: the Aider worker reads the real repo and the `done_when` is the project's OWN test/typecheck (e.g. `npm test`).

banned_patterns.message: FMath::Rand() is a non-crypto PRNG (weak low bits). For security-sensitive values (tokens/keys) use FGuid::NewGuid() or a CSPRNG.
Placeholder / incomplete body — the snippet looks unfinished.
New third-party dependency include — UE plugin must add NO new third-party deps; use engine APIs.

cpp._note: ON as a FAST (ms) heuristic lint ONLY — brace/paren balance (comment/string-aware), leaked-fence, and banned-pattern checks. It does NOT compile or type-check: an isolated C++ fragment can't resolve project/engine headers, and a full native build is too slow per-delegate. REAL C++ verification stays on the batched host-harness (see host_harness config) + the orchestrator's own review. banned_patterns is tunable — grow it from recurring corrections.

metering._comment: §6.5 per-delegation cost/outcome log -> metrics/metrics.jsonl (local, git-ignored). Report: `python src/metering.py [N]`. Claude token counts aren't visible to the pipeline; the tracked signal is machine-verified %% up and Claude step-ins down.

budgets._note: Optional per-tier ceilings (tokens_out). Absent/0 = no cap. Enforcement is advisory in §6.5; the §6.1 retry cap already bounds runaway loops.

agent._comment: File-aware worker agents (Aider in a disposable git worktree). PROJECT-AGNOSTIC: the `assign` MCP tool takes repo=<abs path> + done_when=<shell cmd>, so it works on ANY git repo. Per-repo overrides via <repo>/.qwen-pipeline.json (its `agent` block merges over this). Set aider_exe to your isolated Aider venv's executable in config/qwen.local.json (e.g. .aider-venv/Scripts/aider.exe on Windows).

gemini._note: Used by the `assign` tool (Aider/litellm). model ids MUST use litellm's `vertex_ai/` prefix (service-account/ADC), e.g. `vertex_ai/gemini-2.5-pro` — NOT `gemini/` (that is the AI-Studio API-key path and ignores the service account). Fill models.flash/pro in config/qwen.local.json; the server injects GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS/VERTEXAI_PROJECT/VERTEXAI_LOCATION into env at call time from providers.gemini.

host_harness._comment: OPTIONAL, PROJECT-SPECIFIC batched build/test verify (NOT per-delegate). `python src/host_verify.py [--tests]` builds a host project and optionally runs its tests headless — a heavier 'Tier-2' check to run after a batch of native work / before commit. The example below is an Unreal Engine host project; set your real engine + project paths in config/qwen.local.json (gitignored) so this committed file stays portable. Leave empty to disable.

cascade._comment: §6.4 cost-ordered escalation. When the requested tier still FAILS the §6.1 mechanical gate after gate.max_retries, the pipeline escalates to `escalate_to` IF that provider is enabled (providers.<name>.enabled). Otherwise it falls through to Claude. Gemini is gated on creds today, so escalation is a no-op until providers.gemini.enabled=true. The 'front gate' (which tasks skip the cascade and go straight to Claude) is a Claude-side routing decision — see config/routing.md §6.
